phenomenon: automatic priming of behavior-congruent concepts during media exposure
claim: Stimulus content activates goal-congruent knowledge which subsequently guides behavior independent of reward structure or explicit instruction.

phenomenon: socioeconomic-status-mediated achievement divergence across language groups
claim: Apparent language-group differences in reading growth reflect confounded SES rather than language status when proficiency is held constant.
Trait nostalgia proneness predicts emotion-memory intensity across contexts, but this baseline tendency is moderated by contextual retrieval cues and personality dimensions.

phenomenon: sequence-dependent cognitive-control reconfiguration following high-demand task states
claim: Preceding high cognitive demands trigger upregulation of control processes that enhance subsequent performance, extending conflict-adaptation findings beyond response-conflict to maintenance and interference domains.

phenomenon: inverse social-class relationship with prosocial resource allocation
claim: Lower social class individuals act more prosocially because economic dependence fosters egalitarian values and heightened compassion sensitivity.

phenomenon: temporal asymmetry in moral judgment consistency
claim: Identical actions receive harsher moral judgment when imagined as future rather than past due to stronger anticipatory emotion, revealing systematic temporal inconsistency that disappears under direct comparison.
